summary:
- Monomer-to-polymer conversion involves an increase in density.
- Volume reduction can reach up to 21% due to tighter packing of molecules.
- Final density post-conversion typically ranges between 0.9 to 1.19 g/cm³ depending on specific materials.

Claim: Conversion of monomers to polymers results in higher density and reduced volume.
Evidence: Density increases from around 0.9 g/cm³ pre-polymerization to 1.19 g/cm³ post-polymerization; volume contracts significantly, often by up to 21%.  
Answer: C
